steps.title: Set up the expression
steps.content: There are 7 stations total. If $x$ stations use Experiment A (9 tsp each), then $7 - x$ stations use Experiment B (5 tsp each).

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm C
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘7x’: Treats $x$ as the total salt per station regardless of experiment, ignoring that Experiment A and Experiment B have different teaspoon amounts and that the $7 - x$ Experiment B stations each contribute 5 tsp. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘14x’: Adds $9 + 5 = 14$ and multiplies by $x$, incorrectly applying both per-station rates to the same $x$ stations and omitting the $5(7 - x)$ contribution from the Experiment B stations entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘$4x + 35$’: Matches the simplified expression exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘$14x + 35$’: Adds $9 + 5 = 14$ and multiplies by $x$, then also adds the 35 tsp from 7 Experiment B stations, so it double-counts instead of using $9x + 5(7 - x)$. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
